answer:
Supabase Auth uses two primary schemes for API access [1]:

API Key Authentication: Public endpoints (e.g., /signup, /token) require an apikey header containing either the anon (public) or service_role (private) key [2][1].

Bearer Token Authentication: User-specific endpoints (e.g., /user) require a valid JWT access token provided in the Authorization header [2][1].

To authenticate via the REST API for a password-based sign-in, send a POST request to the /token endpoint [2][3]:

```bash
curl -X POST 'https://<PROJECT_REF>.supabase.co/auth/v1/token?grant_type=password' \
  -H "apikey: YOUR_ANON_KEY" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"email": "user@example.com", "password": "password1"}'
```

After obtaining an access token, include it in the Authorization header for subsequent requests to secure your database and API endpoints [2].
